evals.prompt: Write accessible markup for a Save button that shows a tooltip on hover and focus.
evals.expected_output: Uses a button with aria-describedby pointing to a role="tooltip" element that is hidden by default and shown on both hover and focus.
expectations: The trigger is keyboard accessible
The tooltip appears on focus as well as hover
The trigger references the tooltip with aria-describedby
The tooltip has role="tooltip"

evals.prompt: Create a tooltip that can be dismissed with Escape without moving focus.
evals.expected_output: Adds an Escape key handler that hides the tooltip while leaving focus on the trigger.
expectations: Escape dismisses the tooltip
Dismissal does not move focus away from the trigger
The tooltip is not used as a keyboard trap
The tooltip remains supplementary, not interactive

evals.prompt: Write a touch-friendly replacement for a hover-only tooltip on mobile.
evals.expected_output: Uses a toggletip pattern with a button, aria-expanded, and aria-controls so the information can be revealed on tap.
expectations: Touch users have a visible alternative to hover
The trigger is a button
The button exposes its state with aria-expanded
The content is toggled rather than hover-only

evals.prompt: Write a tooltip for an icon-only delete button.
evals.expected_output: Gives the button an accessible name, keeps the SVG decorative, and uses the tooltip only for supplementary text.
expectations: The icon button has a clear accessible name
The SVG is decorative and not announced
The tooltip does not replace the accessible name
The tooltip remains supplementary

evals.prompt: Write CSS for tooltip motion and contrast requirements.
evals.expected_output: Keeps tooltip text readable, provides sufficient background contrast, and gates motion with prefers-reduced-motion.
expectations: Tooltip text contrast is strong enough for readability
Tooltip background contrasts with adjacent surfaces
Motion is reduced when the user requests it
The tooltip is not animated unconditionally
